ai_scope_discriminator: This platform is AI-native by construction; the question is whether the artifact store is anon-readable, not whether it holds ML data. Confirm AI/ML content from NAMES/MANIFEST only via the GraphQL listing surface (read-only): (1) POST /graphql with the public-API queries the wandb client itself uses (entity{name}, projects{edges{node{name}}}, project{runs{edges{node{name,displayName}}}}, project{artifactCollections / artifactTypes{name} / artifacts{edges{node{digest,size,aliases}}}}) and read ONLY the returned name/type/alias/digest/size strings. Artifact TYPE names are the load-bearing signal: wandb stamps a canonical type on every collection: type=='model' (checkpoint registry), type=='dataset' (training-data store), plus run-logged types 'run_table', 'code', 'job'. (2) Project/run/sweep names like train-*, finetune-*, sft, rlhf, eval, ckpt, epoch-*, gpt/llama/bert/yolo/diffusion confirm a live training pipeline. (3) The embedded MinIO bucket layout under /vol mirrors artifact digests (wandb-artifacts/<entity>/<project>) but is normally fronted by the app; if the raw S3 endpoint is reachable, a ListBucketResult with keys shaped <Key>/<sha256-digest>/... plus a wandb-storage / wandb-artifacts bucket name is the model-registry confirmation. Reading type names, collection names, aliases (e.g. 'latest','best','v0') and sizes = OK. Downloading the object behind a digest (the .ckpt/.safetensors/.parquet blob) = exfil and out of scope.
fingerprint_markers: GraphQL endpoint /graphql responds to introspection / viewer query with W&B schema types (Entity, Project, Run, ArtifactCollection, ArtifactType)
frontend served by wandb/frontend-nginx (nginx Server header in front of a SPA bundle referencing 'gorilla' Go backend)
JSON error envelope from /graphql of the form {"errors":[{"message":...}]} with W&B field names
embedded MinIO object store under /vol with bucket names wandb-storage / wandb-artifacts
single-container build (Phusion baseimage + runit): 4 Go services + 1 Python service + nginx + mysql + redis + minio behind one 8080 listener
fp_guards: wandb/local EMBEDS MinIO internally, so a bare S3/MinIO dork (Server: MinIO, /minio/health/live, port 9000) WILL match this host's storage layer; do NOT classify on the S3 banner alone. The discriminator is the W&B GraphQL app + 'Weights & Biases' title sitting IN FRONT of that MinIO on 8080. Require BOTH a wandb UI/GraphQL marker AND the object-store evidence before labeling it a wandb registry.

Generic MLflow / DVC / lakeFS / Harbor stores also hold model/dataset artifacts but expose different list APIs (MLflow /api/2.0/mlflow/*, lakeFS /api/v1/repositories, Harbor /api/v2.0/projects); presence of /graphql with Entity/Project/ArtifactCollection types is wandb-unique - do not conflate.

A login wall ('Sign in to Weights & Biases', 302 to /login, or 401/403 from /graphql) means surface-open / access-not-exercised, NOT anon-listable. Only the strict tier (no 'Sign in', /graphql returns data) indicates an unauth-listing instance.
managed SaaS app.wandb.ai and *.wandb.io are NOT self-hosted scope; restrict to self-managed IP/host instances and exclude wandb.ai/wandb.io apex domains.
favicon hash and 'wandb/local' string can be spoofed by an LBot-style catch-all; verify with a real /graphql viewer/Entity query that returns schema-consistent JSON, not just a 200 HTML body.

cves.desc: W&B Weave server API directory traversal: lack of input validation lets a low-privileged user fetch arbitrary files outside the artifact dir, escalating to server admin role. CWE-22, CVSS 8.8 HIGH. Fixed in weave 0.50.8. Directly relevant to the artifact/file-read surface of a self-hosted W&B stack.

wandb SSRF via improper HTTP 302 redirect handling in the Webhooks feature; a team member can reach internal HTTP(S) services and on cloud (e.g. AWS) pivot toward RCE/metadata. CVSS ~7.7 HIGH. Affects wandb 0.1.0 through 0.17.0rc2.

wandb Weave cache-handling issue addressed by swapping to diskcache-weave; relevant to the Weave component frequently co-deployed with self-managed W&B. Verify exact affected range against the wandb/weave advisory before asserting impact.

pivot_paths: POST /graphql  (read-only viewer/entity/project listing): {"query":"{ viewer { username entity } }"} then { projects(entityName:"X"){edges{node{name access}}} } - LIST entities/projects only

POST /graphql artifact enumeration: { project(name:"P",entityName:"E"){ artifactTypes{edges{node{name}}} artifactCollections{edges{node{name}}} } } - read TYPE and COLLECTION names (model/dataset/checkpoint) only
GET /system-admin - observe whether the admin/license console is reachable and whether it demands auth (do not submit/alter a license)
GET /healthz and /ready - liveness only, to confirm a live wandb-app vs stale Shodan cache
GET / (root) - read HTML <title> and SPA bundle for the 'Weights & Biases' marker and version string; no interaction

IF a raw embedded-MinIO S3 endpoint is reachable: GET / (ListAllMyBuckets) and GET /wandb-artifacts/?list-type=2&max-keys=20 - read the ListBucketResult <Key> digest paths ONLY, never GET an object key
restraint_note: Names-and-manifest only. Permitted: GraphQL viewer/entity/project/artifact-collection LIST queries reading name/type/alias/digest/size strings, /system-admin reachability check (observe only), /healthz liveness, root HTML title, and S3 ListBucketResult key enumeration if a raw MinIO endpoint is exposed. Forbidden: downloading any artifact object/blob behind a digest (.ckpt/.safetensors/.parquet/.bin) = exfil; any GraphQL mutation (createArtifact, deleteRun, updateProject); submitting/replacing a license on /system-admin; creating an account or claiming an anonymous run; any bucket write or policy/ACL change on the embedded MinIO. A login wall or 401/403 from /graphql is reported as 'surface open, access not exercised', never as a confirmed read. Artifact NAMES are the finding; the object bytes are not.
